documents.text: NEVADA ORTHOPEDIC & SPINE CENTER 7455 W. Washington Ave Suite 160 Phone: (702) 878-0393 1505 Wigwam Pkwy Suite 330 Las Vegas, Nevada 89128 www.nevadaorthopedic.com Henderson, Nevada 89074 Edward L. Carriere, JR MRN#: 3433690 DOB: 04/04/1948, 77 years Date: 03/12/2026 Orthopedic Evaluation History of Present Illness The patient is a 77 year old female who presents for a lower extremity evaluation. The condition involves the right lower extremity. Additional reasons for visit: Transition into care is described as the following: The patient is transitioning into care from another physician and a summary of care was not provided. Patient presents to clinic today with problems related to his right leg. He has chronic lymphocytic leukemia which has been known for several years. He had an episode where he bumped his leg recently and had onset of pain. A fracture was diagnosed and it was found to be within the lesion. Subsequent imaging has been completed and he wishes to be evaluated for the etiology and treatment options for the lesion. He has an aching sensation in the leg with weightbearing. He denies weight loss. He is at his baseline level of health otherwise. Examination: Head, ears, eyes, nose, and throat: Atraumatic and normocephalic. Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation. Skin: No open lesions, rashes or wounds. No café-au-lait spots. No dark or hairy patches present. Vascular: Normal distal pulses all limbs. Capillary refill less than 2 seconds all limbs. No adenopathy present. No stasis changes about ankles. Normal finger and toenails. Neurologic: Cranial nerves II - XII grossly intact. Light touch intact all limbs. Motor exam is 5/5 upper and lower extremities. Gait pattern is normal. Knee: Limb length symmetric. Patella tracking in the mid line. No patella apprehension sign. No effusion present. No crepitus with motion. No joint line tenderness. 1A Lockman's exam bilaterally. No McMurray's either knee. Range of motion normal. Right leg examination shows that he is moderately tender over the lateral aspect of the leg with a palpable mass in the mid lateral aspect. There is no break in the skin. There is no discoloration. No venous dilatation. He can flex and extend the knee without difficulty. He can flex extend the ankle without difficulty. Plain film radiographs of the tibia and fibula on the right side are reviewed. They show an infiltrative destructive lesion in the mid diaphysis with a transverse fracture that has some bridging callus visible. MRI of the leg is reviewed and it shows an expansile destructive lesion with soft tissue extension involving the mid aspect of the fibula. Bone scan is reviewed and it was equivocal, moderate increase in activity in the right lower extremity. Impression: 1. Chronic lymphocytic leukemia. 2. Right fibula infiltrative destructive lesion concerning for sarcoma. 3. Pathologic fracture right fibula. Recommendation and medical decision making: The patient is 77 years old and we discussed the fact that I think he has a sarcoma and that chemotherapy is not a good option at his age due to the neurologic complications that can occur. Printed: 03/27/2026 11:28:46 AM Page 1/3 In light of these facts I am recommending radical excision of the mid aspect of the fibula for diagnostic and therapeutic purposes. Once tissue diagnosis has been obtained then we can decide whether radiation therapy will be necessary for local disease control. We discussed the general behavior of sarcomas. We discussed the fact that the differential diagnosis would be an infection but this has a very aggressive appearance for an infection with no history of prior trauma direct inoculation. In anticipation of surgery he is given a letter requesting cardiac risk stratification. Follow-up for preop. A total of 50 minutes was spent in direct contact with the patient interviewing the patient, examining the patient, reviewing imaging studies with the patient, reviewing reports with the patient and formulating this treatment plan. Reviewed HPI, History and ROS at today's visit. The dictated information, contained herein, was transcribed with voice recognition software. Although reviewed, the content may contain errors and/or omissions. Patient History Allergies No known drug allergy [] Allergies Reconciled Medications amLODIPine (10mg tablet, oral) Active. rosuvastatin (20mg tablet, oral) Active. valACYclovir (500mg tablet, oral) Active. clopidogreL (75mg tablet, oral) Active. HYDROcodone-acetaminophen (5-325mg tablet, oral) Active. lidocaine HCl-hydrocortison ac (3-0.5% cream, topical) Active. gabapentin (400mg capsule, oral) Active. Medications Reconciled. Past Surgical Urinary and/or Bladder Surgery Phreesia 03/09/2026 Gallbladder Surgery Phreesia 03/09/2026 Cancer Surgery Phreesia 03/09/2026 Prostatectomy Phreesia 03/09/2026 Social Tobacco use: Former smoker >120 years Phreesia 03/09/2026 Disability Status: Not Disabled Phreesia 03/09/2026 Current work status: Not Currently Employed Phreesia 03/09/2026 Hand Dominance: Right Handed Phreesia 03/09/2026 Assistance Status: No Home Assistance Available Phreesia 03/09/2026 Surgical Blood Transfusion: Approves surgical blood transfusion Phreesia 03/09/2026 Alcohol use: None Phreesia 03/09/2026 Contract: No Active Pain Contract Phreesia 03/09/2026 Drug Assessment: No Illicit Drug Use Phreesia 03/09/2026 Family Cancer: Brother Phreesia 03/09/2026 Other Past Medical Patient Has Cardiologist Phreesia 03/09/2026 Hyperlipidemia Phreesia 03/09/2026 Hypertension Phreesia 03/09/2026 Problem List No Known Problems Status is Inactive Problems Reconciled Vitals Printed: 03/27/2026 11:28:46 AM Page 2 / 3 03/12/2026 01:19 PM Weight: 186 lb Height: 68 in Body Mass Index: 28.28 kg/m² Pain Level: 7/10 "BMI >25: Losing Weight" handout given to address weight-loss options and benefits. Review of Systems General Not Present- Chills, Fever, Night Sweats, Persistent Infections, Recurring Infections, Weight Gain and Weight Loss. HEENT Not Present- Hearing Loss, Hoarseness, Visual Disturbances and Wears glasses/contact lenses. Cardiovascular Not Present- Chest Pain, Difficulty Breathing On Exertion, Palpitations, Shortness of Breath and Swelling of Extremities. Neurological Present- Tingling. Not Present- Burning, Decreased Memory, Difficulty Using Hands, Numbness, Seizures, Stroke and Trouble walking. Psychiatric Not Present- Anxiety, Depression, Fearful, Frequent crying and Other Psychological Condition. Hematology Not Present- Easy Bruising and Excessive bleeding. Assessment and Plan Pathological fracture of fibula, initial encounter ( M84.469A) Malignant neoplasm of right fibula ( C40.21) Leukemia, lymphocytic, chronic ( C91.10) Orders Diet Education Authorization for Service - (SURGERY) Additional Instructions Pre-op studies to be completed by PCP Follow up for pre-op Patient Education Handout BMI >25: Losing Weight - Electronically signed by: RONALD W. HILLOCK MD on 03/18/2026 Printed: 03/27/2026 11:28:46 AM Page 3 / 3
